commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action requires a human with a valid fresh Cursor.app session to supply provider-side proof; there is no concrete automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The patch opens the local SQLite database read-only and adds no dependency, downloaded executable, permission expansion, or observable credential logging. (sha=4e85caee36ede5a884dce64d6e9e3e58718bf30b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds validated Cursor.app local authentication as the final Cursor usage fallback, preserving existing cookie/session precedence and reusing the established account and request-quota flow.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new fallback capability rather than fixing behavior guaranteed by current main. Its central validation is a successful exact-head provider request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action requires a human with a valid fresh Cursor.app session to supply provider-side proof; there is no concrete automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix: use Cursor app auth as fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- Add Cursor.app local auth as a final fallback after manual, cached, browser, and stored cookie sources fail.
- Read the first-party bearer token from Cursor's `state.vscdb` and query DashboardService usage/account endpoints.
- Match Cursor 2.1's current protobuf JSON schema: derive plan usage from `includedSpend / limit`, preserve extra spend-limit usage, and reject disabled/missing usage payloads.
- Disable the app-auth fallback during browser login verification so an existing Cursor.app account cannot falsely complete an account switch.

## Test Plan
- [x] `swift test --filter CursorStatusProbeTests` - 35 tests passed
- [x] `swift test --filter CursorLoginRunnerTests` - 3 tests passed
- [x] `make check`
- [x] Branch autoreview - no actionable findings

## Validation
- Inspected Cursor `2.1.0-pre.22.patch.0`'s bundled DashboardService protobuf descriptors and usage UI implementation.
- Confirmed current fields for `GetCurrentPeriodUsage`, `PlanUsage`, `SpendLimitUsage`, and `GetMe`.
- Confirmed Cursor computes plan percentage from `includedSpend / limit`; the earlier synthetic auto/API percentage mapping has been removed.
- A local Cursor.app access token was available, but the current DashboardService request returned HTTP 401. No successful fresh-account live fetch is claimed for this head.

## Merge Blocker
- Obtain one successful redacted DashboardService fetch with a fresh Cursor.app token against the current PR head.
target.author: jackie-qin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
